text: Texas Representative Al Green introducing articles of impeachment against the president. The congressional chamber has to act on that resolution in two legislative days. Green says he wants a ye or nay vote on the articles. However, House Speaker Nancy Pelosi does have other options. It could be tabled, referred to the Judiciary Committee, or essentially ignored. Even if the Democratic-controlled House passes the resolution against President Trump, it would still fall to the Senate to vote on impeachment, and the Senate is controlled by Republicans. For more on this story and an exclusive interview with the four congresswomen targeted by President Trump's tweets, stay tuned to CBS This Morning, beginning at seven, right after News Eight at sunrise.
